title: ranunculus recurvens
intro: Ranunculus recurvens, also known as the creeping buttercup, is a flowering plant species in the Ranunculus genus that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Ranunculus recurvens include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or ointment to harness its medicinal properties, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity.
